Params: {3: 'A full moon bathes the city in a silent, silver glow. On a quiet rooftop, Teddy stands motionless, her eyes fixed on the distant, icy specks of starlight.'}
{3: 'The sound of familiar footsteps approaches from behind, but she doesn\'t turn.'}
{3: 'She lifts the plastic bag in her right hand, its weight testifying to the few cans of synthetic alcoholic electrolyte and black tea inside.'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: 'Yours is the black tea. Not exactly the right time or place for a proper brew, so you\'ll have to make do.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'After passing a can of black tea to Teddy, Moineau settles in beside her and pulls out an electrolytic vape.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'Keep sucking on that thing, and you\'ll need a new pair of lungs before you know it.'}
{3: 'Moineau offers a nonchalant smile, then exhales a plume of vapor.'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: 'It\'s not like there\'s a shortage of bionic parts.'}
{3: 'The can of tea hisses as Teddy opens it. She brings it to her lips for a small sip.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'I thought you weren\'t going to bring that up tonight.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'The body\'s at the HQ morgue. The forensic investigator\'s on it, and Moore\'s writing the report.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'But I doubt they\'ll get anything useful.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'That body was a perfect copy of Christina\'s.'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: 'I was too late. I couldn\'t stop those rioting machines.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'Moore and I see it the same way.'}
{3: 'With those words, a heavy silence falls between them.'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Teddy', 3: 'What was she hiding? Why did she have to run? And why were those machines hunting her?', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'There are too many loose ends. It feels like we\'re missing a layer, something deeper we haven\'t even scratched.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'The cause of the "Derealization Disorder", who\'s controlling the machines, the link between Christina and Norman\'s names and faces... we\'ve got nothing.'}
{3: 'Teddy sighs in resignation and crushes the empty can in her hand.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'He\'s different from both Christina and Norman.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'His wounds were not the same. He...'}
{3: 'The memory of that illusion flashes behind Teddy\'s eyes.'}
{3: 'Moineau\'s drone, the unfurling blade of light, its lethal lunge toward "her."'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: '...His wounds didn\'t show any signs of high-temperature burns.'}
{3: 'She cracks open another can of tea, takes a bracing sip, and finally speaks again.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'Plus, Jack had a normal, verifiable life history in Elysium. He was not someone who just "appeared out of nowhere."'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: 'So you\'re saying he was just an ordinary person caught in the crossfire?'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'I\'ve been thinking... Maybe Christina and Norman weren\'t actually killed by whoever\'s controlling the rioting machines.'}
{3: 'Teddy proposes the hypothesis in a half-joking tone, glancing at Moineau for a reaction as she finishes.'}
{3: 'With the electrolytic vape between her lips, Moineau scratches her head in frustration.'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: 'That just makes this mess even more complicated.'}
{3: 'She rests her chin on her hand, explaining methodically.'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: 'Jack isn\'t a Construct. There\'s no need for special methods against him.'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: 'The rioting machines alone would be more than enough.'}
{3: 'Moineau is about to take another drag from her electrolytic vape when Teddy snatches it from her.'}
{3: 'Teddy raises the device to toss it into the plastic bag, then freezes.'}
{3: 'She pulls it back, her eyes narrowing as she examines it.'}
{3: 'A mischievous grin spreads across Moineau\'s face.'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: 'Ah, never mind. Can\'t have a minor vaping.'}
{3: 'Teddy rolls her eyes, tossing the vape into the bag like it\'s offended her.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: '"Minor"... I\'ve told you, you have no talent for jokes.'}
{3: 'Clouds drift in the distance, slipping away at a pace too slow for the eye to truly capture.'}
{3: 'Moineau stares, her gaze unfocused, and exhales a perfect smoke ring. She watches, silent, as the breeze unravels it into nothing.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'Moineau... why did you become an inspector?'}
{3: 'The purple-haired woman hesitates, carefully weighing her response.'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: '...Perhaps because this is the only place I have.'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: '<phonetic=Moineaux>Sparrows</phonetic> don\'t migrate. Limited flight ceiling, can\'t travel far.'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: 'All I want is to protect Elysium—this city.'}
{3: 'Years ago, a younger Moineau had posed a question to that figure.'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Moineau', 3: 'Father, why is this city called Elysium?', 4: '1'}
{2: '???', 3: 'In Greek mythology, there\'s said to be a paradise called Elysium.', 4: '2'}
{2: '???', 3: 'I hope this place can become a similar sanctuary for those who need it.', 4: '2'}
{2: '???', 3: 'When that day comes, you\'ll be able to run freely here, under every sunrise and sunset.', 4: '2'}
{2: '???', 3: 'You\'ll be like a bird, flying freely.', 4: '2'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: 'Too bad I\'m just an unwinged bird.'}
{3: 'She murmurs, the words too soft for Teddy to catch.'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: 'You got to choose. Becoming a Construct, leaving your family behind... escaping the cage.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'What\'s the difference? I just traded one cage for a slightly larger one.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: '<color=#c77cffff>And now even Babylonia, that "slightly bigger cage," has crumbled to dust.</color>'}
{3: 'A stabbing pain pulses through her M.I.N.D. again.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: '<color=#c77cffff>Humanity clawed its way out of the Great Depression into a Golden Age. The World Government was barely established.</color>'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: '<color=#c77cffff>Then someone decided to start a global nuclear war. The surface was scorched, and the surviving cities went into lockdown.</color>'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: '<color=#c77cffff>So, Eden II became the sanctuary in the sky, with the elites fighting for a spot on their glorious Noah\'s Ark.</color>'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: '<color=#c77cffff>But even the Ark sank in the end. Ironic, isn\'t it?</color>'}
{3: 'Her face is a stark, smileless void.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'So go ahead and mock the very idea of "cages."'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'If you want to fly, then fly. You\'re a sparrow, after all.'}
{3: 'A hearty laugh escapes Moineau, sparked by Teddy\'s words.'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: 'You know what? That is such a "Teddy" thing to say.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'Not everyone sees the world as darkly as you do.'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: 'Well, I suppose I could never match your particular brand of sharp-tongued superiority.'}
{3: 'A wave of even louder laughter overtakes Moineau, leaving her breathless.'}
{3: 'When it passes, her face goes still, the mirth draining away to reveal a perfect, unsettling neutrality.'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: 'You were just this tiny thing, tucked away in holographic cyberspace, teaching yourself to code all alone.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'Stop talking to me like some wise elder. It\'s gross.'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: 'Not everyone can break free from their cages.'}
{3: 'Moineau pulls out another electrolytic vape, her next words lost to a sharp inhale.'}
{3: 'Without waiting for Teddy\'s response, she\'s at the rooftop\'s edge, turning to face Teddy.'}
{2: 'Moineau', 3: 'Let\'s call it a day. I\'m not going to sacrifice my nightlife to more work.'}
{1: '1', 3: 'Moineau tips backward into the void, her body swallowed by the night. Her final words and a ghost of vapor are all that remain, lingering for a moment around Teddy before dissipating.'}
{3: 'But then, the falling bird soars upward, borne aloft by her drones that carry her into the distance.'}
{3: 'Teddy remains perfectly still, a statue watching the drones\' fading light trails until they are extinguished by the dark. Only then does a hint of sorrow enter her eyes.'}
